source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/1. HAD1717MC/5. Non-clinical considerations/010_Non-clinical Report.pdf

document.title: Non-clinical Report For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Non-clinical report for the HAD1717MC X-ray Detector providing FDA 510(k) guidance-aligned SSXI testing information and IEC 62220-1 DQE/MTF-related measurements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports non-clinical considerations for solid-state X-ray imaging device submission content, including physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety, and test result characteristics.
keyPoints: Non-clinical testing data was provided in conformance to FDA guidance for 510(k)s for solid-state X-ray imaging devices.
The report identifies IEC 62220-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 as the standard for DQE-related detector measurements.
HAD1717MC is described as a static X-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
The active area is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm with 3052 x 3052 active area pixels.
The report includes measured DQE, MTF, NPS, linearity, sensitivity, lag, pixel defect, and reuse/readout characteristics.

concepts.evidence: Describe how x-ray beam alignment and collimation to the “active” area of the device will be achieved to meet the requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.

evidence.claim: The document is a non-clinical report for the HAD1717MC X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-clinical Report For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC

evidence.claim: The report number is A-PR-0-001 with revision 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-PR-0-001 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The revision date is Sep/20/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. Date Sep/20/2019

evidence.claim: The revision history lists revision 0 as established on Sep/20/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. Rev. date Revision contents Remark 0 Sep/20/2019 Establishment

evidence.claim: The non-clinical testing data was provided in conformance to FDA guidance for 510(k)s for solid-state X-ray imaging devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-clinical testing data was provided in conformance to the FDA “Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)’s for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices”
evidence.pageTarget: Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices

evidence.claim: The report identifies IEC 62220-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 as an applicable standard.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 IEC 62220-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The detector entire dimensions are 470 mm x 470 mm including the outer short ring.
evidence.evidenceText: Entire dimensions include outer short ring 470 (X) mm x 470 (Y) mm

evidence.claim: The detector panel size is 445 mm x 445 mm with the outer short ring removed.
evidence.evidenceText: Panel size (outer short ring removed) 445 mm x 445 mm

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC active pixel area is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC has 3052 x 3052 active area pixels, totaling 9,314,704.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 3052 x 3052 = 9,314,704

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate • 1 fps

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC uses CsI and Gadox scintillator types.
evidence.evidenceText: There are two types of scintillator which are CsI and Gadox.

evidence.claim: The scintillator converts X-ray energy directly to light.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray absorber(scintillator) is commercially available and converts x-ray energy directly to light.

evidence.claim: The absorber wavelength is 540 nm in the visible region.
evidence.evidenceText: This absorbers wavelength is 540nm(Visible region).

evidence.claim: Image data is transmitted and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 16-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two-dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC is a static X-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: Measured HAD1717MC DQE values include 47.8% at 0.1 lp/mm, 39.8% at 0.5 lp/mm, 31.9% at 1.0 lp/mm, 23.5% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 18.9% at 3.0 lp/mm in one DQE section.
The DQE measurement procedure described in IEC 62220-1 was used.
evidence.evidenceText: The measurement procedure described in the report IEC 62220-1 was used

evidence.claim: The detector pitch is 0.14 mm, corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.

evidence.claim: The report states artifacts due to aliasing will be negligible.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible.

evidence.claim: The report gives a lag actual measurement of 0.000171.
evidence.evidenceText: Lag = ((a-b)-(c-d)) / ((b+d)/2) / Actual measurements : 0.000171

evidence.claim: The active area is full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The detector input is designed to be installed perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.

evidence.claim: Readout after X-ray exposure requires at least 2 seconds and image readout, transfer, and preprocessing require under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 2 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure. Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: The report states the stability time is 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Stability time : 5 sec.

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC regulatory approvals section references safety report DRMCBL1909-0016 and EMC report DREKCEE1905-0342.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1717MC meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the HAD1717MC safety report: DRMCBL1909-0016, EMC report: DREKCEE1905-0342

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC electromagnetic compatibility section references IEC standards as outlined in safety report DRMCBL1909-0016 and EMC report DREKCEE1905-0342.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1717MC meets the IEC standards as outlined in the HAD1717MC safety report: DRMCBL1909-0016, EMC report: DREKCEE1905-0342

evidence.claim: The test pattern exposure condition was RQA 5, 70 kV, 200 mA, SID 150 cm, Al 21 mm, and 200 ms.
evidence.evidenceText: Exposure Condition - RQA 5 : 70kV, 200mA, SID : 150cm, Al 21mm - 200ms

evidence.claim: The report lists HAD1717MC sensitivity as 152.55 LSB/uGy.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensitivity Samsung S4343-W 159.95 LSB/uGy HAD1717MC 152.55 LSB/uGy

evidence.claim: The final DQE table lists HAD1717MC DQE values of 70.10% at 0.1 lp, 57.40% at 0.5 lp, 52.10% at 1 lp, 38% at 2 lp, and 28.40% at 3 lp.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: the source states conformance to FDA 510(k) guidance, but it is a manufacturer non-clinical report and does not show FDA issuance, clearance, or approval.
Page count metadata appears inconsistent, with page 1 shown as Page 1/31 and later pages shown as Page 2/29 through Page 29/29.
Some formulas and extracted text show OCR or conversion artifacts, including malformed equations and repeated Korean text '변경된 필드 코드'.
Regulatory approvals and EMC sections reference report numbers DRMCBL1909-0016 and DREKCEE1905-0342 but do not identify the specific regulatory or IEC standards met.
